clause.title: Weatherproofing for external step-free entrance
clause.p: Weatherproofing for an external step-free entrance must be provided in accordance with one or a combination of the following:

ol.li: Where the external surface is concrete or another impermeable surface, a channel drain that meets the requirements of Volume Two H2D2 is to be provided for the width of the entrance.

Where the external trafficable surface is decking or another raised permeable surface, a drainage surface below the trafficable surface is to be provided that meets the requirements of Volume Two H2D2, and drainage gaps in the trafficable surface, such as those between decking boards, are to be no greater than—
8 mm: or
in a designated bushfire prone area, that which is permitted by AS 3959.
A roof covering an area no smaller than 1200 mm by 1200 mm, where the area is provided with a fall away from the building not steeper than 1:40.
The provisions of Clause 2.4 do not apply to an entrance door that is provided through an interconnected garage.
A channel drain provided in accordance with (a) can also act as an inspection zone for the purposes of termite management provisions provided the inspected zone required by AS 3660.1 can be accessed.
Consideration should be given to the ability for cleaning drains in (a), particularly in bushfire prone areas.

For the purposes of (c), any posts, columns, or structural supports for the roof cover must not encroach the clear space required by Clause 1.1(4) for a landing or entrance path provided under Clause 1.1.
